mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding corporate output and revenue acceleration, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive monthly changes in INDPRO fail to coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%, subsequent equity returns over the next 60 trading days turn negative as restrictive monetary policy contracts earnings multiples.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and industrial production indicate a coordinated demand-supply contraction that amplifies downside risk to corporate profits, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and INDPRO do not produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of concurrent down-months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated unemployment above 6 percent, month-over-month accelerations in payroll employment signal recovering labor demand and lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through consumption expectations.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, month-over-month PAYEMS increases fail to correlate with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC above 0.05 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in real retail sales signals stronger consumer demand and translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through earnings optimism.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS changes coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales concurrent with a falling federal funds rate indicate demand expansion amplified by monetary easing, producing strongly positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising RSAFS and falling FEDFUNDS does not outperform the sum of individual signals in predicting positive 20-day returns over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth relative to the prior quarter predicts positive 60-day S&P 500 returns as operating leverage improves corporate margins.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter INDPRO growth fails to exhibit positive Rank IC with 60-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts reflects strengthening construction activity and durable goods demand, driving positive 20-day S&P 500 returns through cyclical sector earnings.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in HOUST do not coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of rising-start months over a 3-year period.
